

# 🔍 ** Incident Report **

##  📁 Incident Summary

* 🏷️ Incident Name: RDP Brute Force Attack

* ⚠️ Severity: Medium
 - **Reason:**
-- Repeated authentication failures were observed.
-- No successful authentication occurred.
-- No evidence of persistence or lateral movement.

* 🛑 Status: Investigation Completed

* 🖥️ Platform: Microsoft Sentinel

* ☁️ Environment: Azure Windows Honeypot

* ⏰ Detection Time: Automatically generated by Microsoft Sentinel Analytics Rule.

---

# 💼 Executive Summary

* 📉 A large number of failed Remote Desktop Protocol (RDP) authentication attempts were detected against an Internet-facing             Windows virtual machine deployed as a honeypot in Microsoft Azure.

* 🌐 The attack originated from multiple public IP addresses distributed across different geographical regions.

* 🔒 No successful compromise occurred during the investigation.

---

# 🔍 Detection Method

* 🛡️ Microsoft Sentinel

* 📋 Windows Security Events

* 🆔 Event ID 4625

---

# 🚨 Indicators of Compromise (IOC)

• 📍 Source IP Address

• ❌ Failed Login Attempts

• 👤 Username Targeted

• 🗺️ Geographic Origin

---

# 🕵️‍♂️ Investigation Findings

* 🔨 The attacker attempted repeated password guessing against exposed RDP services.

* 📊 Analysis of authentication logs showed repeated login failures originating from several countries.

* 🌍 Geo-IP enrichment helped identify attacker locations.

* 🛡️ No evidence of successful authentication was observed.

---

# ⚠️ Risk Assessment

* 📈 Likelihood:** High

* 💥 Impact:** Medium

* 📊 Overall Risk:** Medium

---

# 💡 Recommendations

• 🚫 Disable public RDP exposure

• 🔐 Enable Multi-Factor Authentication

• 🧱 Restrict RDP using NSGs

• 🔒 Implement account lockout policies

• 👁️ Monitor authentication failures continuously

---

# 🧠 Lessons Learned

* ⚠️ Even temporary Internet exposure results in automated brute-force attacks.

* 📊 Microsoft Sentinel provides effective centralized visibility for authentication monitoring.

* 📝 Structured investigation and documentation improve incident response consistency.
